title: Game Loop — Main Loop and Save
description: Wrap the game in a main loop so the player can keep exploring until they choose to quit or die. Use a while loop or do-while loop that continues as long as the player is alive and hasn't quit. When the player quits, save their stats to a file using ofstream. Include a loop counter or turn number that increments each turn. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'while' in cpp or 'do' in cpp, 'Use a while or do-while loop for the main game loop'
assert 'ofstream' in cpp, 'Use ofstream to save game data to a file'
assert '#include <fstream>' in cpp, 'Must include <fstream> for file output'
import re
loop_match = re.search(r'while\s*\(', cpp)
assert loop_match, 'Must have a while loop condition (e.g., while (playing && health > 0))'
assert 'playing' in cpp or 'gameOver' in cpp or 'quit' in cpp or 'running' in cpp, 'Use a boolean variable to control the game loop'
assert '<<' in cpp and ('save' in cpp.lower() or 'ofstream' in cpp), 'Write player data to the save file using <<'
acceptance_criteria: Game runs in a while or do-while loop
Loop continues while player is alive and hasn't quit
A turn counter increments each iteration
Player can quit the game to exit the loop
Game stats are saved to a file using ofstream when quitting
Save file includes player name, health, gold, and turns played
hints: while (playing && health > 0) { ... } is a good loop condition
Use turn++ at the start of each loop iteration
ofstream saveFile("savegame.txt"); saveFile << playerName << endl; saveFile << health << endl;
Don't forget to close the file or let the destructor handle it
Check if the player typed 'quit' to set playing = false
